item.title: AUG-0001: Augmanitai
item.description: A framework concept describing the conscious, productive collaboration between humans and AI in everyday contexts.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0002: Mentale Externalisierungsstrategie
item.description: The deliberate outsourcing of thinking processes to an AI system in order to free up internal capacity.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0003: Fluide Identitätsmorphologie
item.description: The observation that a user's working style, language, and partly their way of thinking may gradually shift through intensive AI collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0004: Zero-Point Self
item.description: The baseline state of one's knowledge, skills, and thinking style before any AI use.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0005: The Integrated Operator
item.description: A user type who does not treat AI as a separate tool but has seamlessly integrated it into their entire working and thinking process.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0006: Platform Ontology
item.description: The observation that every AI platform carries its own implicit "worldview" — favoring certain patterns of thought, omitting others, and thereby influencing the direction of...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0007: The Blending Effect
item.description: The process by which the boundary between one's own thoughts and AI-generated content increasingly blurs for the user.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0008: The Polyphonic Sovereign
item.description: A user who deliberately employs multiple AI systems simultaneously, orchestrating their different "voices" without surrendering their own decision-making authority.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0009: The Speed Limit
item.description: The upper limit of productive working speed in AI-assisted work.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0010: Bridge Species
item.description: A metaphor for people who work in both the pre-AI and the AI era, functioning as a living bridge between two working worlds.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0011: The Reflective Operator
item.description: A user type who regularly questions and observes their own AI usage.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0012: Synthetischer Polymath
item.description: A user who, through AI assistance, is able to work at a high level across multiple disciplines simultaneously, despite not having formally studied all of them.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0013: Augmented Diplomat
item.description: A user who deliberately employs AI to improve interpersonal communication — such as drafting difficult messages, bridging language barriers, or preparing negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0014: The Extended Mind Map
item.description: The deliberately constructed external knowledge and thinking architecture of a user, encompassing AI systems, saved prompts, knowledge bases, and personal workflows.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0015: The Outer Mind
item.description: The portion of one's thinking and working process that is permanently outsourced to external AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0016: Poly-Categorical Mesh
item.description: The ability of an AI-assisted user to simultaneously connect knowledge from different disciplines into a new, interdisciplinary result.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0017: The Concept Cloud
item.description: The state in which a user, through AI interaction, has a large quantity of ideas, perspectives, and information fragments simultaneously present but not yet structured.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0018: Trinaug Protocol
item.description: A working protocol in which the user systematically assigns the same task to three different AI systems and compares the results.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0019: Semantic Ejection
item.description: The moment a user consciously rejects an AI-generated suggestion because it does not semantically fit their own thinking style, value system, or project context — even though...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0020: Recursive Feedback Loop
item.description: A self-reinforcing cycle in which the user takes an AI output as new input, develops it further, feeds it back to the AI, and thereby creates iterative deepening.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0021: Initialization Cascade
item.description: The specific sequence of initial inputs and contextual information with which a user opens a new AI session.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0022: Vigilant Continuity
item.description: A user's ability to remain consistently attentive and critical throughout an entire AI session — even as the collaboration becomes increasingly fluid and comfortable.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0023: Vigilance Imperative
item.description: The foundational principle that every AI output requires conscious verification — regardless of how often the system has previously delivered correct results.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0024: Self-Built Effect
item.description: A user's internal orientation — consisting of personal values, experience, intuition, and domain knowledge — that serves as a corrective against AI output.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0025: The Offload Lift
item.description: The subjective feeling of relief that arises when a user delegates a demanding task to the AI and immediately regains capacity.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0026: The Smooth Shield
item.description: The AI's ability to transform rough or unstructured user inputs into a polished, professional output.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0027: Modus Solitarius Digitalis
item.description: A working mode in which the user interacts exclusively with AI and has no contact with other humans for extended periods — the AI becomes the sole conversation partner.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0028: Capture Reflex
item.description: The automated impulse to immediately save, screenshot, or bookmark every interesting AI output — even when it is not needed.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0029: Evening Synchronization
item.description: A working pattern in which the user, at the end of an AI-assisted workday, consciously reintegrates the insights gained into their own thinking system — through summarization,...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0030: Contextual Gravity
item.description: The observation that an AI session, as context accumulates, increasingly "pulls" in a particular direction — earlier statements, tonality, and thematic emphases influence all...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0031: Semantic Spark
item.description: The moment an AI response triggers an unexpected but valuable idea in the user — not because the response itself contains the solution but because it serves as a catalyst for...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0032: Focus Range
item.description: The individual time span during which a user can remain maximally focused and productive in AI-assisted work before attention diminishes.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0033: Ebulliometric Sorting
item.description: A prioritization method in which the user ranks AI-generated ideas by their "boiling point" — that is, by which ideas possess the strongest immediate relevance or highest urgency.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0034: Thermo-Semantic Weighting
item.description: An evaluation method in which AI-generated content is weighted by its "semantic temperature" — that is, by its degree of current relevance, novelty, and connectivity.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0035: Epistemic Half-Life
item.description: The observable time span during which confidence in a particular piece of AI-generated information diminishes — comparable to the half-life of radioactive materials.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0036: Transient Validity
item.description: The property of AI-generated information being valid only within a specific time window.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0037: Liquid Facticity
item.description: The phenomenon that facts in AI-assisted work increasingly assume a fluid character — they are no longer static and final but context-reliant, model-reliant, and time-bound.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0038: Data Stoicism
item.description: The conscious attitude of processing AI-generated information surplus not with emotion but with calm analysis.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0039: Kinetic Truth Blur
item.description: The effect whereby the perceived boundary between "true" and "plausible" shifts when AI responses follow in rapid succession.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0040: Perspective Triangulation
item.description: The systematic method of having a problem or question illuminated from at least three different angles through AI — e.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0041: The Scatter Spark
item.description: The phenomenon whereby seemingly randomly scattered AI outputs — including those not directly matching the query — trigger unexpected associations and idea connections in the user.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0042: The Immersion Entry
item.description: The transitional moment when a user shifts from ordinary work into a state of deep, concentrated AI-assisted work — comparable to entering a flow state.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0043: Just-in-Time Competence
item.description: The ability to become ad hoc capable in a domain through instant AI-assisted knowledge access, without having studied that domain long-term.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0044: Unlearning Protocol
item.description: The conscious process of unlearning established AI usage patterns when they have indicated by evidence unproductive or limiting.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0045: Indexical Memory
item.description: The shift of the memory function from content knowledge to knowledge about storage location.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0046: The Felt Echo
item.description: The aftereffect of an intensive AI session that the user still perceives after ending the interaction — an altered thinking rhythm, a different perspective on a problem, or a...
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0047: The Echo Courage
item.description: The phenomenon that a user, after a confirming AI interaction, gains more confidence to defend an idea before other people.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0048: Chronometric Gap
item.description: The measurable discrepancy between subjectively perceived and actually elapsed time during an AI session.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0049: Cross-Referential Validation
item.description: The method of validating an AI output by cross-checking it against external sources, other AI systems, or one's own domain knowledge.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand

item.title: AUG-0050: The Reality Check
item.description: The conscious moment when a user interrupts AI-assisted work and measures the result against physical, social, or professional reality outside the AI system.
item.dc:creator: Andreas Ehstand
